pattern: Sign flips move freely between any two cells, so only the parity of negative entries limits the result; the answer is the total of absolute values minus twice the smallest absolute value when an odd count remains.
hints: Flipping two adjacent cells toggles two signs, so over the whole grid you can relocate any pair of negative signs.
If the number of negative entries is even you can clear them all and sum every absolute value.
With an odd negative count one cell must stay negative; sacrifice the entry with the smallest absolute value, i.e. subtract twice that minimum from the absolute-value total.

edge_inputs.description: odd negatives force one residual negative on smallest magnitude
a zero absorbs the leftover sign for free
smallest grid all negative even count

stress.description: square grids of random signed values exercising both parity cases
stress.note: random signs hit even and odd negative counts and zeros
